title: Androcles
narrative_elements: slave escapes and heals wounded lion in forest
lion shelters and feeds androcles in cave
both captured and sentenced to arena spectacle
lion recognizes androcles and shows affection, leading to his pardon

title: The Ant and the Chrysalis
narrative_elements: ant mocks imprisoned chrysalis for its immobility
chrysalis endures taunts without reply
chrysalis transforms into a butterfly
butterfly flies away leaving ant behind

title: The Ant and the Grasshopper
narrative_elements: grasshopper sings and plays all summer while ant works
ant collects food and ignores grasshopper's invitation to idle
winter arrives and grasshopper faces starvation
ant has stores of food while grasshopper suffers

title: The Ass and His Shadow
narrative_elements: traveler hires ass to travel on a hot day
both men dispute ownership of the ass's shadow
quarrel escalates from words to blows
ass runs away while men fight

title: The Ass and the Frogs
narrative_elements: ass carrying wood stumbles and falls in a pond
ass groans heavily under his load
frogs mock the ass for complaining about a temporary fall

title: The Ass and the Lapdog
narrative_elements: ass envies the pampered lapdog and its easy life
ass breaks free and imitates lapdog's playful behavior
ass destroys dishes and jumps on master
servants beat ass back to stable

title: The Ass and the Lapdog
narrative_elements: lapdog frisks and dances for farmer's affection
ass breaks loose and imitates lapdog's antics
ass tries to climb into farmer's lap
servants drive ass back to stable with sticks

title: The Ass and the Mule
narrative_elements: ass begs mule to share part of his load on steep mountain path
mule refuses to help the overburdened ass
ass collapses and dies under his load
mule must carry both loads plus ass's hide as consequence

title: The Ass in the Lion's Skin
narrative_elements: ass finds and dons a lion's skin
all animals and people flee in fear from disguised ass
ass brays and reveals his true identity
owner beats ass for causing fright

title: The Ass's Brains
narrative_elements: fox lures ass to lion with promise of royal alliance
lion kills ass and instructs fox to guard the prey
fox secretly eats ass's brains while lion sleeps
fox deflects lion's anger with wit about ass's lack of brains

title: Avaricious and Envious
narrative_elements: jupiter grants wishes with condition neighbor gets double
avaricious man wishes for gold but envies neighbor's double amount
envious man wishes to lose one eye so neighbor goes completely blind

title: The Bald Knight
narrative_elements: wind blows off knight's hat and wig while hunting
companions burst into laughter
knight laughs along and makes witty remark about borrowed hair

title: The Bald Man and the Fly
narrative_elements: fly repeatedly stings bald man on his bare head
man swats at fly and hits himself instead
man learns to restrain himself and stops striking

title: The Bat, the Birds and the Beasts
narrative_elements: war breaks out between birds and beasts
bat wavers and sides with each group alternately
peace is made and both groups reject the bat
bat is left with nowhere to belong

title: The Bear and the Two Travelers
narrative_elements: bear suddenly appears on the travelers' path
one traveler climbs a tree and hides
other traveler plays dead and bear sniffs him and leaves
surviving traveler tells friend bears advise never traveling with cowards

title: Belling the Cat
narrative_elements: mice council meets to discuss how to outwit the cat
young mouse proposes tying a bell around the cat's neck
plan is met with approval
old mouse asks who will actually bell the cat and silence falls

title: The Belly and the Members
narrative_elements: body members rebel against belly for being idle
members refuse to do their work
entire body becomes debilitated
members repent their folly too late

title: The Birdcatcher, the Partridge, and the Cock
narrative_elements: birdcatcher has no food and needs to feed a guest
partridge pleads its usefulness as a decoy
cock pleads its usefulness as morning alarm
birdcatcher kills cock anyway for dinner

title: The Bowman and Lion
narrative_elements: bowman shoots an arrow at the challenging lion
lion flees in fear from the arrow wound
fox urges lion to show courage
lion reasons that the arrow is more fearful than the man himself

title: The Boy and the Filberts
narrative_elements: boy grabs as many filberts as he can from a pitcher
hand gets stuck because he grabbed too many
boy cries in frustration
bystander advises him to take only half

title: The Boys and the Frogs
narrative_elements: boys throw stones at frogs in a pond for sport
several frogs are killed
one frog raises its head and pleads for them to stop
frog points out that what is sport for boys is death for frogs

title: The Boy and the Nettles
narrative_elements: boy is stung by a nettle after touching it gently
boy runs home to tell his mother
mother advises him to grasp nettle boldly next time
lesson: bold action prevents the sting

title: The Buffoon and the Countryman
narrative_elements: buffoon imitates animal sounds and is praised
countryman challenges him saying it is nothing like a real pig
countryman performs with real pig pinched behind back
audience hisses the countryman but he reveals the real pig

title: The Cat-Maiden
narrative_elements: jupiter transforms a cat into a maiden to settle dispute with venus
cat behaves like a proper woman at wedding feast
venus releases a mouse to test the cat
cat instinctively leaps after mouse revealing her true nature

title: The Cat and Venus
narrative_elements: cat falls in love with young man and asks venus to transform her
venus transforms cat into a woman
venus tests the transformed cat by releasing a mouse
cat chases mouse revealing unchanged nature and is reverted

title: The Cat and the Mice
narrative_elements: cat hunts mice one by one in an infested house
mice retreat to their holes out of fear
cat pretends to be dead to lure mice out
mouse sees through the ruse and refuses to come near

title: The Cock and the Pearl
narrative_elements: cock struts in farmyard and finds a shining pearl in straw
cock acknowledges the pearl's worth to those who value it
cock declares he would prefer a single barleycorn to a peck of pearls

title: The Crow and the Pitcher
narrative_elements: thirsty crow finds pitcher with water too deep to reach
crow tries multiple times to access water and fails
crow drops pebbles one by one into pitcher
water rises until crow can finally drink

title: The Dancing Monkeys
narrative_elements: prince trains monkeys to dance in rich clothes
monkeys perform well and receive applause
courtier throws nuts on stage to cause mischief
monkeys forget dancing and fight over nuts revealing their true nature

title: The Dog and the Cook
narrative_elements: house dog invites a street dog to feast on master's food
invited dog revels in anticipation of a grand meal
cook discovers and throws invited dog out the window
ejected dog pretends he drank too much wine to save face

title: The Dog in the Manger
narrative_elements: dog claims an ox's manger for his nap
ox returns from work and tries to eat its straw
dog rages and barks preventing ox from eating
ox gives up hope and goes away hungry

title: The Dog and the Shadow
narrative_elements: dog carries piece of meat across a plank over a brook
dog sees his own shadow in water thinking it is another dog with meat
dog snaps at shadow to steal the meat
dog drops its meat into the water and loses everything

title: The Eagle and the Arrow
narrative_elements: eagle watches a hare from lofty rock
hidden archer shoots eagle with mortal wound
eagle sees arrow is feathered with his own feathers
eagle laments dying by arrow made from his own wings

title: The Farmer and the Cranes
narrative_elements: cranes eat seeds from farmer's newly-sown wheat fields
farmer scares cranes with empty sling but they learn it is harmless
cranes ignore the farmer's empty threats
farmer loads sling with stones and kills many cranes causing rest to flee

title: The Farmer and the Snake
narrative_elements: farmer finds frozen snake and warms it in his bosom
snake revives and bites the farmer mortally
farmer dies lamenting his misplaced compassion

title: The Farmer and the Stork
narrative_elements: farmer traps cranes robbing his fields and catches a stork too
stork pleads his good character and broken leg for mercy
farmer refuses to spare stork because he was caught with the thieves

title: The Fisher and the Little Fish
narrative_elements: fisherman catches only a tiny fish after a full day
small fish pleads to be released promising to grow bigger
fisherman refuses saying he has the fish now and may not catch it later

title: The Fisherman Piping
narrative_elements: fisherman plays flute hoping fish will dance into his net
fish do not respond to music
fisherman casts net the ordinary way and catches many fish
fisherman scolds fish for dancing now that he has stopped piping

title: The Four Oxen and the Lion
narrative_elements: lion repeatedly fails to attack four oxen who stand together
oxen quarrel and separate to different parts of the field
lion attacks each one individually
lion defeats and kills all four oxen one by one

title: The Fox And The Bramble
narrative_elements: fox grabs bramble bush for support while climbing fence
brambles scratch fox's paws painfully
fox complains about the bramble's cruelty
bramble explains it always grabs others and cannot help it

title: The Fox and the Cat
narrative_elements: fox boasts of having a hundred tricks for escaping enemies
cat admits to having only one trick but relies on it
hounds approach and cat climbs tree to safety
fox deliberates too long and is caught and killed

title: The Fox, the Cock and the Dog
narrative_elements: fox tells cock a false truce has been declared between beasts and birds
cock pretends to be interested and mentions an approaching dog
fox immediately retreats fearing the dog hasn't heard of the truce
cock's wit exposes fox's deceptive scheme

title: The Fox and the Crane
narrative_elements: fox invites crane to dinner and serves soup in a flat dish
crane cannot eat from the dish and goes hungry
crane invites fox to supper and serves food in a narrow-necked flagon
fox cannot taste the food and receives a fitting lesson

title: The Fox And The Raven
narrative_elements: raven steals cheese and perches in a tree
fox flatters raven on his elegant appearance and voice
raven caws to show off and drops the cheese
fox grabs cheese and mocks raven for having voice but no brains

title: The Fox and the Crow
narrative_elements: crow carries piece of cheese up to a tree branch
fox flatters crow on her looks and voice
crow opens beak to sing and drops cheese
fox snaps up cheese and departs

title: The Fox and the Goat
narrative_elements: fox falls into a deep well and cannot escape
fox persuades thirsty goat to jump in by praising the water
fox climbs out using goat's back and runs away
fox berates goat for jumping in without thinking of how to get out

title: The Fox and the Hedgehog
narrative_elements: injured fox lies in a ravine covered with blood-sucking flies
hedgehog offers to drive away the flies
fox refuses saying flies are already full and not stinging much
fox explains hungry flies would drain all remaining blood

title: The Fox and the Woodcutter
narrative_elements: fox fleeing hounds begs woodcutter for a hiding place
woodcutter hides fox but points to the hut when huntsman asks
huntsman believes the words not the gestures and rides on
fox leaves without thanks and rebukes woodcutter for betrayal in heart

title: The Frogs Asking for a King
narrative_elements: frogs petition jupiter for a king and receive a harmless log
frogs grow contemptuous of the log and petition for another king
jupiter sends an eel who is also too meek
jupiter finally sends a heron who preys on the frogs until none are left

title: The Goatherd and the Wild Goats
narrative_elements: goatherd shelters wild goats together with his own during snowstorm
goatherd feeds wild goats more to entice them to stay
wild goats flee when thaw comes
wild goat explains excessive favor for new arrivals warns against trusting him

title: The Goose With the Golden Eggs
narrative_elements: countryman finds his goose lays golden eggs daily
countryman grows rich but becomes greedy and impatient
countryman kills goose expecting to find all gold at once
goose is dead and no gold is found

title: The Hare and the Hound
narrative_elements: hound chases hare from his lair for a long time
hound gives up the chase
goat-herd mocks hound saying the smaller animal ran better
hound explains hare ran for its life while he ran only for dinner

title: The Hares and the Frogs
narrative_elements: hares decide to end their lives due to constant fear
hares stampede to a lake to drown themselves
frogs hear the noise and flee into deep water
hare realizes there are creatures even more timid and stops friends

title: The Hare With Many Friends
narrative_elements: hare is popular and claims many friends among other beasts
hounds approach and hare asks each friend in turn for help
each friend makes excuses and declines to help
hare must rely on her own speed to escape

title: The Hart and the Hunter
narrative_elements: hart admires his grand antlers while drinking at a pool
hart wishes his legs were worthier of his noble crown
hunter approaches and hart flees with his swift legs
hart's antlers catch in tree branches allowing hunter to catch up

title: The Hart in the Ox-Stall
narrative_elements: hart fleeing hounds hides in ox-stall buried in hay
hunters ask stable boys who look but miss the hart
master notices unusual things sticking from hay
stable boys discover and kill the hart

title: The Hawk, the Kite, and the Pigeons
narrative_elements: pigeons fear the kite and ask hawk for protection
hawk agrees and is admitted into the pigeon coop
hawk kills more pigeons in one day than kite could in a year

title: The Heifer and the Ox
narrative_elements: heifer taunts ox for being forced to labor
owner releases ox from yoke at harvest festival
owner binds heifer for sacrifice
ox smiles and tells heifer she was spared for this purpose

title: Hercules and Pallas
narrative_elements: hercules encounters a strange monster and beats it
monster grows three times larger with each blow
hercules beats it harder and monster fills the road
pallas reveals monster is strife and it shrinks when left alone

title: Hercules and the Wagoner
narrative_elements: carter's wagon wheels sink into a rut
carter does nothing and cries to hercules for help
hercules appears and orders carter to put his own shoulders to the wheels
hercules warns that prayers are vain without self-help

title: The Horse, Hunter and Stag
narrative_elements: horse asks hunter to help exact revenge on the stag
hunter requires horse to accept bit and saddle to pursue stag
horse agrees and together they defeat the stag
hunter refuses to remove saddle and bridle saying he prefers horse tame

title: The Hunter and the Woodman
narrative_elements: hunter searches for lion tracks in the forest
woodman offers to show him the lion itself
hunter turns pale and refuses saying he wanted only tracks not the lion

title: The Huntsman and the Fisherman
narrative_elements: huntsman and fisherman chance to meet and exchange their day's catch
both are delighted with the exchange
they repeat the exchange day after day
neighbor warns repeated exchange will destroy the pleasure of novelty

title: The Kites and the Swans
narrative_elements: kites and swans both have the gift of song
kites hear the horse neigh and are enchanted
kites try to imitate the neighing sound
kites forget how to sing while trying to neigh

title: The Labourer and the Nightingale
narrative_elements: labourer captures nightingale wanting it to sing for him always
nightingale says it cannot sing in a cage
nightingale offers three pieces of wisdom in exchange for freedom
labourer frees it and nightingale flies away

title: The Lark Burying Her Father
narrative_elements: lark was created before the earth existed
lark's father dies with no earth for burial
lark keeps father unburied for five days
lark buries him in her own head explaining her crest

title: The Lark and Her Young Ones
narrative_elements: farmer asks friends then relatives to help harvest his wheat
lark tells young ones not to worry as long as farmer relies on others
farmer finally decides to harvest himself with his own son
lark immediately moves the nest saying now is the time to go

title: The Lion and the Eagle
narrative_elements: eagle asks lion to form an alliance
lion is not opposed but asks for surety of good faith
lion points out eagle can fly away from bargains whenever he likes
lion refuses to trust someone who can so easily flee

title: The Lion, the Fox, and the Ass
narrative_elements: lion fox and ass share a hunting agreement
ass divides spoils equally into thirds and is devoured by angry lion
fox gives lion nearly everything keeping almost nothing for himself
lion praises fox's division and fox says he learned from ass's fate

title: The Lion, the Mouse, and the Fox
narrative_elements: mouse runs over sleeping lion's mane waking him
fox mocks lion for being startled by a tiny mouse
lion denies being afraid of mouse scratching him
lion claims he was concerned mouse might mess up his mane

title: The Lion's Share
narrative_elements: lion fox jackal and wolf hunt together and kill a stag
lion claims the first quarter as king of beasts
lion claims second quarter as arbiter and third for participating in hunt
lion dares anyone to touch the fourth quarter and fox slinks away

title: The Lost Wig
narrative_elements: lion wearing a wig tries to impress a tiger across the street
wind blows off wig leaving lion bald and embarrassed
lion laughs it off and makes a witty remark about borrowed hair

title: The Man and His Wife
narrative_elements: man's wife is hated by all household members
man sends her to her father's house to test her effect there
wife returns saying even herdsmen who barely see her dislike her
man concludes those who spent all day with her must have hated her even more

title: The Man and His Two Wives
narrative_elements: young wife plucks man's white hairs to make him look younger
old wife plucks his black hairs to make him look older
both wives pull hairs in opposite ways for their own interests
man ends up completely bald as consequence

title: The Man and the Satyr
narrative_elements: man and satyr form an alliance over drink
man blows on hands to warm them on cold day
man later blows on food to cool it
satyr refuses friendship with one who blows both hot and cold

title: The Man and the Lion
narrative_elements: man and lion argue over who is stronger while traveling
man points to a statue of a man strangling a lion as proof
lion counters the statue was made by a man not a lion
lion says lion-made statues would show the opposite

title: The Mice and the Weasels
narrative_elements: weasels repeatedly defeat mice in battle
mice elect generals and create a disciplined army
mouse generals wear straw ornaments on their heads as insignia
mice are routed again and generals are caught because of their ornaments

title: The Milk-Woman and Her Pail
narrative_elements: milk-woman fantasizes about profits from milk to buy eggs to raise chickens
plans expand to buying a gown and rejecting suitors at Christmas
she tosses head imagining rejecting suitors
pail falls and spills ending all imaginary plans

title: The Miller, His Son, and Their Ass
narrative_elements: miller tries to please each group of critics who comment on their journey
changes how they carry the ass based on each person's opinion
they end up trying to carry the ass themselves on a pole
ass falls into river and is lost while trying to please everyone

title: The Mountains in Labour
narrative_elements: mountains shake crash and smoke causing great alarm
countrymen gather in fear expecting something horrible
everyone waits in great suspense
tiny mouse emerges from the gap in the mountain

title: The Mother and the Wolf
narrative_elements: wolf hears mother threaten to throw child to wolf to quiet it
wolf waits all day for mother to carry out her threat
mother fondly soothes child at evening and threatens to kill the wolf
wolf goes home cold and hungry cursing himself for believing a woman

title: The Mule
narrative_elements: well-fed mule gallops about boasting of his noble racing father
mule claims to be swift and spirited like a horse
mule is driven on a long hard journey and becomes weary
mule concludes his father must have been just an ass

title: The Nurse and the Wolf
narrative_elements: wolf hears nurse threaten to throw child to wolf
wolf waits patiently outside window for child
child finally cries and wolf comes forward eagerly
nurse shuts window and calls dogs who chase wolf away

title: The Oak and the Reeds
narrative_elements: great oak is uprooted by wind and falls across a stream
fallen oak asks how weak reeds survive strong winds
reeds explain they bend before every breath while oak fights the wind
reeds say their flexibility is why they remain unbroken

title: The One-Eyed Doe
narrative_elements: blind doe grazes near cliff edge watching land with good eye for danger
doe turns injured eye toward sea expecting no danger there
boatmen pass by sea and shoot her mortally
doe laments that the very shore she thought safe proved most perilous

title: The Peacock and Juno
narrative_elements: peacock petitions juno for a nightingale's voice
juno refuses the request
peacock persists citing his status as juno's favourite bird
juno explains each creature has its appointed gifts

title: The Peasant and the Apple-Tree
narrative_elements: peasant resolves to cut down barren apple tree sheltering birds and insects
creatures beg him to spare the tree promising to sing for him
peasant ignores pleas and continues cutting
peasant finds honey in hollow tree and decides to preserve it

title: The Quack Frog
narrative_elements: frog claims to be a learned physician able to heal all diseases
fox asks how frog can prescribe for others
fox points out frog cannot heal its own lame gait and wrinkled skin

title: The Raven and the Swan
narrative_elements: raven envies swan's white plumage and desires it for himself
raven believes swan's whiteness comes from bathing in water
raven abandons his food source and moves to lakes and pools
raven cannot change his color and dies from lack of food
